overview.tagline: A welcoming borough with small-town charm, excellent schools, and a thriving Boulevard
overview.description: Hasbrouck Heights is a classic Bergen County borough known for its charming Boulevard business district, strong community spirit, and convenient location just minutes from MetLife Stadium and major highways. With a mix of well-maintained homes, top-rated schools, and a walkable downtown, the borough offers a quintessential suburban New Jersey experience with easy access to New York City.
highlights: The Boulevard — vibrant main street with local shops and restaurants
Annual Hasbrouck Heights Street Fair draws thousands of visitors
Excellent public schools with strong academic programs
Minutes from MetLife Stadium and American Dream mall
Convenient access to Route 17, I-80, and the NJ Turnpike

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. The borough periodically conducts revaluations to align assessed values with current market conditions.
realEstate.recentTrends: Hasbrouck Heights has seen steady price appreciation driven by its walkable downtown, school quality, and proximity to NYC. Inventory remains tight, and homes in the central Boulevard area command premium prices.
neighborhoodNotes: Boulevard area — Walking distance to shops, restaurants, and train; most desirable location
North end — Quieter residential streets near Teterboro border, larger lots
South end — Family-friendly neighborhood near Depken Field and schools
West side — Close to Route 17 shopping and dining options

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Hasbrouck Heights Department of Public Works.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days by neighborhood zone
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through the DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic #1-#7) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same day as recyclables
Recycling bins available through the DPW
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through December
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided by Suez Water (now Veolia) and sewer service is managed by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia (formerly Suez Water New Jersey)
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia on a quarterly basis
Sewer fees included on the borough tax bill
Water quality reports available online through Veolia

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board of Adjustment
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and significant renovations

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough operates municipal parking lots and manages street parking along the Boulevard.
details: Free municipal parking lots available on side streets off the Boulevard
Metered parking along portions of the Boulevard — 2-hour limit
Commuter parking permits available for NJ Transit bus riders
Overnight parking restrictions enforced on borough streets
Snow emergency parking bans in effect during storms

additionalServices.title: Hasbrouck Heights Free Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library offering books, digital resources, programs, and community spaces.
details: Located at 320 Boulevard
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Free Wi-Fi and computer access
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues including baseball, soccer, basketball, and football
Summer recreation programs and camps
Senior citizen programs and events
Depken Field complex with athletic fields and facilities

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Hasbrouck Heights enforces noise regulations to maintain the peaceful character of its residential neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Excessive noise from gatherings subject to police enforcement after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition, including lawns, structures, and sidewalks.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must not exceed 6 feet in rear yards and 4 feet in front yards
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed from property
Properties must be kept free of debris and refuse

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Hasbrouck Heights requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws throughout the borough.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Barking dogs subject to noise complaint enforcement
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the borough with specific rules for residential and commercial areas.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM without permit
2-hour parking limit in metered zones along the Boulevard
Snow emergency parking bans enforced — vehicles must be moved off-street
Commercial vehicles over 4 tons prohibited from parking on residential streets
Handicapped parking violations subject to significant fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and inspected to ensure compliance with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy of rental units
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Rental property inspections conducted at change of tenancy
Multi-family dwellings subject to additional fire safety requirements

other.title: Garage & Yard Sales
other.summary: Garage and yard sales are permitted in Hasbrouck Heights with certain restrictions.
keyRules: Permit required from the Borough Clerk for garage and yard sales
Limited to a maximum number of sales per year per household
Signs must be removed promptly after the sale ends
Sales hours typically restricted to daytime hours

publicSchools.name: Hasbrouck Heights High School
publicSchools.notes: Strong academics with a variety of AP courses, competitive athletics, and active extracurricular programs

publicSchools.name: Hasbrouck Heights Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Located on the same campus as the high school; offers a comprehensive middle school curriculum

publicSchools.name: Corpus Christi Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Neighborhood elementary school serving the eastern portion of the borough

publicSchools.name: Euclid Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the western portion of the borough

publicSchools.name: Lincoln Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school centrally located in the borough

privateSchools.name: Corpus Christi School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic parish school with a long history in Hasbrouck Heights

nearbyHigherEd: New Jersey Institute of Technology (Newark) — 25 minutes

whyMoveHere: Walkable downtown Boulevard with local shops, restaurants, and services
Strong community identity with active civic organizations and events
Convenient highway access to Routes 17, 46, I-80, and the NJ Turnpike
Affordable compared to many Bergen County towns with good schools
Close to MetLife Stadium, American Dream, and major shopping centers
NJ Transit bus service providing direct access to NYC

commuteToNYC.duration: 30-50 minutes to Port Authority Bus Terminal
commuteToNYC.notes: Several NJ Transit bus routes provide service from the Boulevard to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown Manhattan. Rush hour service is frequent. Driving to nearby Secaucus Junction for train service is another option.

costOfLiving.summary: Hasbrouck Heights offers a moderate cost of living for Bergen County, with housing prices below the county median and reasonable property taxes relative to services provided.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 15% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Walkable Boulevard with local character and amenities
More affordable than many Bergen County towns
Excellent highway access for commuters driving to NYC or elsewhere
Strong sense of community with annual events and active organizations
Good public schools with small-town feel
cons: Aircraft noise from nearby Teterboro Airport
No direct train service — bus commute required for NYC
Route 17 traffic can impact local roads during peak hours
Smaller lot sizes typical of older Bergen County boroughs
Property taxes still relatively high compared to national averages

restaurants.name: Ivy Inn
restaurants.description: Classic American restaurant and bar offering steaks, seafood, and comfort food in a warm, traditional setting. A Hasbrouck Heights institution.

restaurants.name: Houlihan's
restaurants.description: Popular chain restaurant with a strong local following, offering American fare, cocktails, and a lively bar scene.

restaurants.name: Hasbrouck Heights Pizza
restaurants.description: Beloved local pizzeria serving New York-style pizza, calzones, and Italian favorites for takeout and delivery.

restaurants.name: Perks Cafe
restaurants.description: Cozy neighborhood cafe on the Boulevard serving specialty coffee, pastries, sandwiches, and light meals.

restaurants.name: Archer & Goat
restaurants.description: Modern gastropub offering craft cocktails, elevated pub fare, and a curated beer selection in a stylish atmosphere.

parks.name: Depken Field Complex
parks.description: The borough's main athletic complex with baseball diamonds, soccer fields, basketball courts, and a walking path. Home to many of the borough's youth sports leagues.

parks.name: Woodland Park
parks.description: Quiet neighborhood park with walking paths, a playground, and shaded picnic areas.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Memorial park honoring local veterans, featuring monuments, benches, and a reflective setting near Borough Hall.

events.name: Hasbrouck Heights Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair along the Boulevard featuring vendors, food trucks, live entertainment, rides, and family activities. One of the borough's biggest community events.
events.when: September, typically a Sunday in mid-September

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual Memorial Day parade through the Borough honoring veterans and active military, followed by a ceremony at Veterans Memorial Park.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday tree lighting ceremony at Borough Hall with carols, hot cocoa, and a visit from Santa Claus.

attractions.name: The Boulevard
attractions.description: Hasbrouck Heights' charming main street lined with local shops, restaurants, salons, and professional offices. The heart of the community's commercial life.

attractions.name: Aviation Hall of Fame & Museum of New Jersey
attractions.description: Museum at nearby Teterboro Airport showcasing New Jersey's aviation history with vintage aircraft, artifacts, and interactive exhibits.

attractions.name: Hilton Hasbrouck Heights
attractions.description: Full-service hotel offering dining, event spaces, and accommodations convenient to MetLife Stadium and NYC.

shoppingAreas: The Boulevard — Main shopping street with local businesses
Route 17 corridor — Big-box retail and national chains

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 8:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 320 Boulevard, Hasbrouck Heights, NJ 07604

hospitals.name: Hackensack Meridian Health — Pascack Valley Medical Center

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
